ddls:
CREATE TABLE "departments" (
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each department */,
  "department_name" TEXT /* Name of the department */,
  "department_head_id" INTEGER /* ID of the department head */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("department_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_departments_department_head_id FOREIGN KEY ("department_head_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_roles" (
  "job_role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job role */,
  "job_role_name" TEXT /* Name of the job role */,
  "job_role_description" TEXT /* Description of the job role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("job_role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "employees" (
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each employee */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the department the employee belongs to */,
  "job_role_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the job role of the employee */,
  "satisfaction_level" INTEGER /* Satisfaction level of the employee (1-4) */,
  "last_evaluation" REAL /* Last performance evaluation score (0-1) */,
  "average_monthly_hours" INTEGER /* Average monthly work hours */,
  "time_spend_company" INTEGER /* Time spent in the company (years) */,
  "work_accident" INTEGER /* Whether the employee has had a work-related accident (0/1) */,
  "left" INTEGER /* Whether the employee has left the company (0/1) */,
  "salary" INTEGER /* Salary level (1-3) */,
  "manager_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee's manager */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the employee */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the employee */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("employee_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_manager_id FOREIGN KEY ("manager_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_department_id FOREIGN KEY ("department_id") REFERENCES departments ("department_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_job_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("job_role_id") REFERENCES job_roles ("job_role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "salary_ranges" (
  "salary_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each salary range */,
  "job_role_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the job role the salary range applies to */,
  "salary_level" INTEGER /* Salary level (1-3) */,
  "min_salary" REAL /* Minimum salary for the range */,
  "max_salary" REAL /* Maximum salary for the range */,
  "currency" TEXT /* Currency of the salary (e.g., USD, EUR) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("salary_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_salary_ranges_job_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("job_role_id") REFERENCES job_roles ("job_role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "employee_performance" (
  "performance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance evaluation */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee being evaluated */,
  "evaluation_date" TEXT /* Date of the performance evaluation */,
  "evaluation_score" REAL /* Score of the performance evaluation (0-1) */,
  "evaluation_comments" TEXT /* Comments on the performance evaluation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("performance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employee_performance_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "work_accidents" (
  "accident_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each work-related accident */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee involved in the accident */,
  "accident_date" TEXT /* Date of the accident */,
  "accident_description" TEXT /* Description of the accident */,
  "accident_severity" TEXT /* Severity of the accident (e.g., minor, major) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("accident_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_work_accidents_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., HR manager, department head, executive) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "training_sessions" (
  "training_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each training session */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee attending the training */,
  "training_date" TEXT /* Date of the training session */,
  "training_topic" TEXT /* Topic of the training session */,
  "training_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the training session (in hours) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("training_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_training_sessions_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)
